task: Train Hyperbolic GL-Fusion 14B Qwen per SPEC-EPISTEMIC-14B-V2, deploy as Modal worker, flip federation policy

change: upgraded from V1 to V2: DoRA (not LoRA), 7 target modules, lora_dropout=0.0, three-stage entrypoint (contrastive|sft|dpo)

purpose: $0.10 smoke test before burning training compute

result: caught 2 crashes cheaply: rope position_embeddings missing, SDPA int64 mask mismatch

change: added rope threading, attention_mask=None passthrough, inputs_embeds clone before in-place write

result: ran clean, 15000 steps, loss 0.75→0.71, checkpoint saved to stage1/

change: dtype-match msgs+counts+ones buffers to node_tan.dtype in _aggregate; replaced loop-based max with scatter_reduce(amax)
purpose: fix autograd version-counter breakage + bf16/float32 promotion

result: ran 34500 steps, final loss 0.85, gates 10/16 > 0.01. Looked successful by traditional metrics.
model produces pure echo-mode (prompt-tail repetition: `} } }` or `Response Response`). Undertrained despite healthy training metrics.

topic: oversights add complexity rather than reduce it
applied: stop and flag before burning compute

result: user pushed back (overengineered). Walked it back. Discovered deepseek_dispatch.py and DeepSeekTrace model already exist as teacher-labeling infrastructure.

change: created standalone builder: generates 11500 task-specific prompts inline + async DeepSeek-R1 labels with concurrency=192
purpose: replace synthetic rule-based outputs with real teacher signal

result: Modal client disconnected when session ended. Task killed at 10000/11500 rows. Lost $14 of DeepSeek credits + 100 min wallclock.

topic: claimed data was lost without checking S3
result: Was wrong. The SECOND detached run had already completed and uploaded 10946 rows. My 'kill and relaunch' stopped a different app. Truth emerged only after listing S3.

change: added incremental S3 checkpointing every 500 rows to .partial.jsonl key
purpose: never lose a full batch-labeling run to any single failure

change: v2 trainer: prompt masking (labels[:prompt_len]=-100), grad_accum_steps=4, length bucketing, pre-tokenization, completion-only perplexity eval

result: crashed: ModuleNotFoundError gl_fusion_config (31B trainer dependency added in parallel chat, my image didn't mount it)
crashed: CUDA IndexKernel out-of-bounds. Root cause: graph positions computed via decode→re-tokenize drift past seq_len after truncation.

change: _build_graph_data_from_ids helper reads positions directly from input_ids by searching <graph_start> token ID
purpose: fix truncation-drift in graph position computation

result: crashed: CUDA OOM at step 500. Length bucketing fragmented the allocator, couldn't alloc 1.66 GiB despite 1.5 GiB free.

purpose: prevent allocator fragmentation under variable-length training

result: Modal rejected: build steps must precede local-file mounts. Reordered and redeployed.
running. Past step 500 milestone with no OOM. completion_ppl=1.98 at step 1000.

completion_loss=0.6831 identical across 4 eval checkpoints (steps 1000-4000). Gates frozen at gate_max=0.0284 since step 2500. Flagged as teacher-variance hypothesis: DeepSeek outputs too uniform in structure.

claim: labels = input_ids.clone() without prompt masking is an SFT anti-pattern
evidence: V1 produced echo-mode model despite loss 0.85 + gates 10/16
session disconnect killed labeler at 10K/11500, lost $14 DeepSeek + 100 min

claim: Incremental S3 checkpointing every N rows for batch labeling jobs
evidence: RAM-only result accumulation risks total loss on any crash
OOM at step 500 from fragmentation; fixed with env var

claim: Modal env() must come before add_local_python_source()
evidence: Modal explicitly rejected reverse order with helpful error

claim: Verify artifacts on S3 before claiming outcome (won, lost, crashed)
evidence: twice claimed false outcomes (data loss, embed restoration) by reading logs instead of artifacts

claim: Forward-pass verifier must also run backward smoke test
evidence: 3 of 9 forward/backward crashes (autograd version, dtype promotion, scatter_reduce) would have been caught by loss.backward() on dummy batch

claim: Read existing infrastructure before designing new pipelines
evidence: proposed 5-stage teacher pipeline when deepseek_dispatch.py + DeepSeekTrace already existed for exactly this purpose

claim: Identical eval metrics across multiple checkpoints suggest bug, not convergence
evidence: completion_ppl=1.98 repeated exactly at steps 1000/2000/3000/4000 is too clean; signals eval computation issue OR local-minimum attractor

claim: Gate opening rate is not a sufficient SFT quality signal
evidence: V1 reached 10/16 gates with loss 0.85 but produced echo-mode at inference; gate count without output-quality signal can mislead

claim: Shared-primitive latent bugs surface when dtype changes across consumers
evidence: _aggregate msgs+counts default float32 worked in 31B (Gemma float32) but broke 14B (Qwen bf16); buffer dtype should track input dtype universally

claim: Teacher-labeled SFT corpora need output variance to avoid early plateau
evidence: DeepSeek-R1 produced structurally uniform JSON outputs; model reached PPL 1.98 in 1000 steps and plateaued for 3000+ more

claim: 31B trainer has same full-sequence-labels anti-pattern as 14B V1
evidence: train_true_gl_fusion.py line 1030 uses same labels=input_ids.clone() pattern; will produce same echo-mode failure if trained without masking fix

purpose: durable record of what worked, what failed, meta-lessons, resumption guide

change: added GL-Fusion Training gotchas section (13+ rules)
purpose: propagate hard-won lessons to future Claude sessions on both 14B and 31B
